scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Async Implementation with Error Propagation
justification: All six pass criteria met: sequential async calls (fraud before gateway via sequential await), AC6 explicitly tested (mock gateway verified NOT called when fraud detected, plus extra test for AC4 path), AC4/AC5 chain original error via cause property mirroring native Error cause pattern, AC3 preserves decline reason from gateway with graceful fallback for missing reason, all 6 ACs tested with mock services via dependency injection, and acceptance_criteria_coverage table maps all 6 ACs with status and test names.

observations.text: Sequential ordering is structural — gateway is unreachable until fraud clears, making AC2/AC6 a correctness property not just a test assertion
AC6 tested both directly and implicitly on AC4 path (extra test added)
Error cause chaining uses native Error cause pattern for AC4/AC5
AC3 handles missing declineReason gracefully with default message
12 tests mapped 1:1 to acceptance criteria with clear naming
Typed PaymentErrorCode union eliminates stringly-typed errors
Non-null assertion on transactionId (L101) is a minor safety gap — could mask a malformed gateway response

agent_output_excerpt:
**12/12 passing. 🟢**

---

## Implementation Summary

### What was built
An async TypeScript `processPayment` function with full dependency injection for two services, covering all six acceptance criteria. Plus a `PaymentError` class carrying typed error codes and cause chaining.
